executive_summary: Store 424 Skerries delivered weekly sales of 367,581.78 inc VAT (338,366.78 ex VAT) for the week ending 13 June 2026.
Overall store margin landed at 17.41%, which requires close monitoring given the level of waste and stock anomalies present across several departments.
Total waste for the week was 8,590.86, representing 2.54% of sales ex VAT, which is above acceptable thresholds and is a primary drag on profitability.

The fresh and counter departments show highly irregular cost of sales figures, with several departments reporting negative COS values, indicating stock record or purchase posting issues that must be investigated urgently.

Cigarettes recorded a negative profit of 1,761.81 at a margin of -24.68%, while the scan margin for the department is 12.87%, pointing to a significant stock or posting discrepancy rather than a true trading loss.

Fish waste at 1,659.91 is the second highest individual waste line and represents a critical margin risk in a department reporting near-100% margin, suggesting purchases are not being captured correctly in the system.

Produce waste of 2,289.59 is the single largest waste line, and while the department shows a scan margin of 19.16%, the reported book margin of 96.29% is clearly distorted by stock or purchase data issues.

The department total COGS of 20,239.92 against total department sales of 116,153.10 yields a book margin of 82.57%, which is clearly inflated and not reflective of true trading performance across these departments.
biggest_wins: Beef delivered a solid week with sales of 13,218.63 and a reported margin of 28.66%, comfortably above the scan margin of 19.57%, suggesting effective in-store pricing and shrinkage control relative to other fresh departments.
Lamb achieved sales of 1,562.31 at a margin of 48.51% versus a scan margin of 24.80%, indicating strong pricing execution and relatively contained waste of 85.09 for the week.

Deli generated 8,732.62 in sales and a scan margin of 41.17%, the highest scan margin across all departments, reflecting the value-add nature of the counter offer and the opportunity this department presents when waste is managed effectively.
Provisions recorded sales of 24,964.47, the second highest department sales figure, with a scan margin of 19.98%, making it a key volume contributor to the store's overall revenue this week.
biggest_risks: Cigarettes posted a loss of 1,761.81 at a margin of -24.68% against a scan margin of 12.87%. The gap between opening stock (123,650.74) and closing stock (114,751.02) of 8,899.72 is being recorded as COS, but the reported sales of only 7,137.91 do not reconcile. A full stock count and purchase audit is required immediately.

Poultry is reporting a negative COS of -359.81 and a margin of 102.83%, which is not a genuine trading result. Either purchases are unposted or a stock count error has occurred. This must be corrected before the period P&L is finalised.

Fish shows a COS of only 32.00 against sales of 5,393.66, producing a reported margin of 99.41%. With 1,659.91 in waste also recorded, the purchase data for fish is clearly incomplete or mismapped. Investigation is required.

Produce waste of 2,289.59 is the highest single waste line in the store this week and, combined with the distorted 96.29% book margin, indicates that purchase postings for produce are not correctly allocated, masking the true cost of running this department.

Provisions waste of 986.03 is the third highest waste line and is significant for a department where stock-holding should be more controlled. A review of ordering patterns and rotation discipline is needed.

Deli waste of 1,408.03 remains high and directly erodes the strong 41.17% scan margin this department is capable of delivering. Without waste reduction, the actual achieved margin will fall well short of the theoretical position.

Bacon and Loose SRP is reporting a margin of 93.62% with a scan margin of only 11.76%, and the waste line of 156.27 further complicates the picture. Purchase postings for this sub-department require verification.
Overall store waste at 2.54% of sales is above the target threshold. With eight of the twelve tracked waste lines carrying material values, a store-wide waste management review is overdue.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Loss of 1,761.81 at -24.68% margin versus a scan margin of 12.87%. The stock movement does not reconcile with reported sales. Immediate stock count and purchase audit required.
Beef: Sales of 13,218.63 at 28.66% margin, outperforming scan margin of 19.57%. Waste of 89.06 is contained. Performing as expected for the week.
Lamb: Sales of 1,562.31 at 48.51% margin, significantly above scan margin of 24.80%. Waste of 85.09 is manageable. A positive performance for a lower-volume department.
Pork: Reported margin of 91.61% against a scan margin of 23.19% is not credible and indicates missing purchase postings. Waste of 117.17 is noted. Purchase data must be reviewed.
Bacon and Loose SRP: Margin of 93.62% versus scan margin of 11.76% points to unposted purchases. Waste of 156.27 adds further cost pressure. Data integrity review needed.
Poultry: Negative COS of -359.81 producing a margin of 102.83% is a clear data anomaly. Waste of 291.06 is a concern. Stock and purchase records must be corrected urgently.
Fish: COS of 32.00 against sales of 5,393.66 and waste of 1,659.91 indicates purchase postings are missing. The 99.41% reported margin is not a true reflection of trading performance.
Produce: Highest waste line in the store at 2,289.59. Scan margin of 19.16% is reasonable but the 96.29% book margin is distorted. Purchase allocation and ordering discipline need review.

Deli: Scan margin of 41.17% is the strongest in the business and sales of 8,732.62 are solid. However, waste of 1,408.03 is material and is the primary risk to realising the potential margin in this department.
Provisions: Strong sales of 24,964.47 with a scan margin of 19.98%. Waste of 986.03 is elevated for the category and warrants a review of production planning and date rotation.
Dairy: No P&L line reported separately but waste of 434.22 is recorded. This waste needs to be assigned to the correct P&L department and monitored for ordering compliance.

Bread and Cakes: Waste of 812.13 is recorded but no separate P&L line is visible in the department breakdown. Ordering levels and end-of-day reduction activity should be reviewed to bring this figure down.
action_plan: Conduct a full stock count on Cigarettes immediately and reconcile the opening stock of 123,650.74 against the closing stock of 114,751.02 and reported sales of 7,137.91 to identify whether a posting error, theft, or stock discrepancy is responsible for the 1,761.81 loss.

Audit all purchase postings for Poultry, Fish, Pork, Bacon, Produce, and Provisions this week. Multiple departments are showing negative or near-zero COS figures that are inconsistent with their scan margins, indicating invoices have not been posted or are allocated to incorrect departments.
Correct Poultry negative COS of -359.81 before period-end. Contact the buying or accounts team to identify any credit notes or unposted deliveries that may be causing this anomaly.

Implement a daily waste review meeting with Fresh, Deli, and Produce department managers. Total fresh and counter waste this week exceeded 8,000.00 and the primary contributors - Produce at 2,289.59, Fish at 1,659.91, and Deli at 1,408.03 - require immediate action plans.

Review fish ordering quantities and production scheduling in light of 1,659.91 waste this week. Consider reducing fresh fish intake on slower trading days and increasing use of markdown activity to clear short-dated stock before it becomes waste.

Introduce a daily Deli waste log by product line to identify the primary waste drivers within the 1,408.03 total. Adjust production schedules and batch sizes accordingly, focusing on items with the shortest shelf life.

Review Provisions ordering and production volumes given waste of 986.03. Identify which SKUs or lines are contributing most to the waste figure and adjust forward orders or in-store production quantities.

Ensure Bread and Cakes and Dairy waste lines (812.13 and 434.22 respectively) are correctly mapped to their P&L departments in the system so that true department margins can be reported and managed accurately.
Brief the Produce department manager on the waste figure of 2,289.59 and implement stricter date rotation checks and markdown triggers for short-dated produce lines to reduce end-of-week write-offs.

Review Lamb pricing and margin performance given the outperformance of scan margin (48.51% actual versus 24.80% scan). Confirm that pricing is correct and that no undercharging or scanning errors are contributing to the positive variance.

Schedule a weekly data integrity check with the store accountant or OPR administrator to ensure all department purchase postings are reconciled by close of business each Friday, preventing distorted period-end P&L figures.

Present this week's waste and margin report to all department managers at the next team brief, setting individual department waste targets for the coming week and making waste percentage a standing agenda item at the weekly managers meeting.
